rows.notes: Clear centered page title. No monetary amounts in the title line; very legible handwriting and placement.
Description legible as 'Civitati Oxon'. Numeric columns align well with the ruling lines; pounds=3, shillings=16, pence=8 clearly formed and within valid ranges.
Abbreviated college label read as 'Coll adj: Xti' (handwriting abbreviation ambiguous but plausible). Amount reads 0 pounds, 0 shillings, 4 pence; digits align cleanly in columns.
Description reads as another abbreviated college line 'Coll adv: Xti' (minor ambiguity in abbreviation). Amount 0-18-0 is clearly written and complies with shillings<20 rule.
Description reads 'Coll omne: anim:' (Latin abbreviation visible). Amount 4-12-4 is crisply written and columns are unambiguous.
Label 'Coll Bal:' clear. Amount 0-12-6 aligned to ruling lines; all digits distinct and valid.
Abbreviated name 'Coll D.J.B.' readable. Amount 0-8-2 cleanly formed and within expected ranges.
Handwriting for the description is somewhat ambiguous — read as 'Mayors Capori' though final word is unclear. Amount 0-14-0 is legible and columns align well.
Description likely 'Curia Hunting' (last word uncertain; could be 'Hunting' or similar). Amount 0-1-8 is clear though small; placement in columns supports reading.

Description transcribed as an overseers line; several place-names present and readable with minor uncertainty. Pence shows a small loop/'ob' indicating a halfpenny — interpreted as 11½d. Columns and fraction symbol are distinct.
Description likely 'Easter Syndals pro ditto' (abbreviations present but reading coherent). Amount 0-7-0 is clear and aligned.
Latin description 'Pro Carbonibus ad Fest: annum' legible. Large round figure 23-0-0 clearly aligned to pounds column; digits are unambiguous.

Indented/subordinate line beginning with dash; wording uncertain ('pro ditto ad such Mail' is a best reading). Amount repeats 23-0-0; numbers are clear but description letters are faded and ambiguous.
Short description reads as 'Coque' (word uncertain). Amount 1-10-4 is legible; alignment to £/s/d lines is good but the description is ambiguous.
Description pale and uncertain; read as 'Loturie' though letters are not crisp. Amount 2-0-0 is clear and satisfies currency validation.
Description interpreted as 'Scull: & Quadrangs:' (some letters stylized). Amount 1-6-8 is readable and within valid ranges; column alignment supports this.
Reads as 'Cleaning Chap: &c: pter' (abbreviations present). Amount 0-2-0 is small but clearly written and aligned to the shillings/pence columns.
Name-like entry read as 'Jonson' (first letter could be G/J; chosen 'J' based on stroke). Amount 0-13-4 is clear and columns are unambiguous.
Label 'Supervision edif:' legible and clearly a payment for building supervision. Amount 4-0-0 is crisp and well aligned.
Clear entry 'Janitor'. Amount 1-14-8 is distinctly written; digits pass currency validation and align to columns.
Reads as 'Bibliothecario' (Latinized form visible). Amount 0-13-4 legible though script slightly faded; alignment supports reading.
Indented/subnote beginning with dash: 'Bibl: Lect: Octr 5' (abbreviations). Amount 0-2-0 clear and in correct columns.
Description 'Castle Prisoners' is readable. Amount 0-10-0 is clear and aligned; digits unambiguous.
Description appears as 'Exeter Sumner (pd poor Newbery)' — inside parentheses is uncertain but reads as distribution to a poor named Newbery. Amount 1-0-0 is clear.
Phrase reads as 'Riddle of Beggars' (could be 'Riddle' or 'Relief' depending on script; chosen 'Riddle' as the lettering matches). Amount 1-3-0 is clearly written.
Final total indicated by symbol and clear numeric entry 74-4-11½. Pence shows the same halfpenny loop as earlier; columns and summation mark are unambiguous.
